name: Calgary
summary: Calgary is one of Canada's safest major cities with low crime rates, excellent public safety infrastructure, and a strong sense of community security. While petty crime exists in downtown areas and transit hotspots, violent crime against tourists is rare.
quickVerdict: Calgary offers a very safe environment for tourists, ranking as one of the safest cities in North America. The city has well-maintained infrastructure, reliable emergency services, and a generally peaceful atmosphere. Crime statistics show Calgary performs better than many comparable cities, with most incidents being minor property crimes rather than violent offenses. Visitors can explore confidently during the day and exercise normal urban caution at night.

neighborhoods.name: Beltline
neighborhoods.description: Vibrant downtown district with excellent nightlife, restaurants and shopping. Well-lit streets with good police presence and heavy foot traffic provide natural security.

neighborhoods.name: Kensington
neighborhoods.description: Charming village-like neighborhood with boutique shops and cafes. Very safe with minimal crime and a strong community atmosphere.

neighborhoods.name: East Village
neighborhoods.description: Recently gentrified area that's become one of Calgary's most hip districts. Generally safe with modern infrastructure and good lighting.

neighborhoods.name: Inglewood
neighborhoods.description: Historic neighborhood with unique shops and galleries. Safe during the day with good community presence, though quieter at night.

neighborhoods.name: Downtown Core
neighborhoods.description: Business district that's very safe during daytime hours but can feel deserted at night. Some homeless population but generally non-threatening.

neighborhoods.name: Forest Lawn
neighborhoods.description: More industrial area with higher petty crime rates. Not particularly dangerous but less ideal for tourists, especially at night.

scams.name: Taxi Overcharging
scams.description: Some taxi drivers may take longer routes or claim the meter is broken to inflate fares.
scams.howToAvoid: Use rideshare apps like Uber or Lyft, ask for meter usage upfront, or agree on fare before starting the trip.

scams.name: Fake Charity Collectors
scams.description: People approach tourists near attractions asking for donations to fake charities.
scams.howToAvoid: Only donate to registered charities, ask for official identification, and verify organizations online before giving.

scams.name: Credit Card Skimming
scams.description: Devices installed at ATMs or payment terminals to steal card information, particularly in tourist areas.
scams.howToAvoid: Use ATMs inside banks, check for loose parts or unusual attachments, cover your PIN, and monitor transactions regularly.

scams.name: Counterfeit Event Tickets
scams.description: Fake tickets sold for Calgary Stampede, sports events, or concerts through unauthorized sellers.
scams.howToAvoid: Buy tickets only from official venues, authorized websites, or verified resellers. Avoid street vendors and social media sellers.

soloFemale.overview: Calgary is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers, ranking among the top destinations globally for women's safety. The city has low harassment rates, excellent public transportation, and a culture of respect and helpfulness. Solo female travelers report feeling comfortable walking alone during the day and reasonably safe at night in well-lit, populated areas.
tips: Stick to well-lit, populated areas when walking alone at night, especially downtown and Beltline
Use reputable rideshare services or licensed taxis rather than walking alone late at night
Trust your instincts and don't hesitate to ask locals for help - Canadians are known for being helpful and polite
Keep emergency contacts saved and consider sharing location with friends when exploring alone
Be aware that some nightlife districts may have occasional unwanted attention, but serious incidents are rare

nightSafety.overview: Calgary maintains relatively good safety levels at night, though the atmosphere changes after dark like any major city. Downtown core and popular areas like Stephen Avenue and Beltline remain well-patrolled and generally safe, while quieter residential areas and industrial zones require more caution. The city has good street lighting and emergency services respond quickly.
tips: Stay in well-lit, populated areas like Stephen Avenue, Beltline, and major entertainment districts
Use reliable transportation options rather than walking long distances alone at night
Be aware of your surroundings and avoid displaying valuables in less busy areas
Plan your route in advance and let someone know your whereabouts
Emergency services (911) are readily available and respond quickly throughout the city

transport.metro: Calgary's CTrain system is generally safe and reliable, with one of the busiest ridership rates in North America. The system operates on an honor system with periodic fare checks. Some caution advised during late night hours when stations are less busy. Security presence and emergency call boxes are available at stations.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Lyft operate safely throughout Calgary with standard safety features. Drivers are background-checked and trips are tracked. Very reliable and safe option, especially for nighttime travel or getting to/from events.
transport.taxis: Licensed taxis are safe and regulated, though watch for potential overcharging. All legitimate taxis have visible licensing and meters. Calgary taxi companies are generally reputable, but rideshare apps often provide more transparent pricing.
transport.tips: Public transit is safe during normal hours but use caution at night. The CTrain has good coverage and is efficient for reaching most tourist areas. Download the Transit app for real-time updates and route planning.

customs: Canadians are famously polite - saying 'please', 'thank you' and 'sorry' frequently is expected and appreciated
Tipping is customary at 15-20% in restaurants, bars, and for service providers like taxi drivers and hairdressers
Queueing (waiting in line) is taken seriously - cutting in line is considered extremely rude
Personal space is valued - maintain arm's length distance in conversations and avoid touching strangers
Remove shoes when entering someone's home, and it's polite to bring a small gift if invited to dinner

health.overview: Calgary has excellent healthcare facilities with Alberta Health Services providing comprehensive medical care. The city has multiple hospitals including major trauma centers, and healthcare quality is very high by international standards.
health.water: Tap water is safe to drink throughout Calgary and meets all health standards. No need to purchase bottled water for health reasons.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required for Calgary beyond routine immunizations. Standard travel health precautions apply.
health.altitude: Calgary sits at 1,045 meters (3,428 feet) above sea level. Most visitors experience no altitude effects, though those sensitive to elevation changes might notice minor adjustment. Climate features cold winters and mild summers with potential for rapid weather changes.

emergency.usEmbassy: US Consulate Calgary: For emergencies call 613-238-5335 after hours. Address: 615 Macleod Trail SE, Suite 1050, Calgary, AB T2G 4T8. During business hours: 403-266-8962

faq.a: Yes, Calgary is very safe for tourists and ranks among the safest major cities in North America. Crime rates are low and the city has excellent emergency services. Most visitors experience no safety issues during their stay.

Calgary is generally safe at night, especially in well-lit, populated areas like downtown, Beltline, and Stephen Avenue. Use normal urban caution, stay in busy areas, and consider using rideshare services for late-night transportation.

faq.q: Is Calgary safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Calgary is exceptionally safe for solo female travelers with high safety ratings. Women report feeling comfortable exploring alone during the day and reasonably safe at night in populated areas. The city has a culture of respect and helpfulness.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Calgary?
faq.a: No areas are particularly dangerous, but exercise extra caution in quieter industrial areas like Forest Lawn at night. Downtown core can feel empty after business hours. Generally, populated tourist areas are very safe.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Calgary?
faq.a: Yes, Calgary's CTrain and bus system are safe and reliable. The CTrain is one of the busiest light rail systems in North America. Exercise normal caution during late hours and keep belongings secure, but the system is generally very safe.
